feed.title: vmargb@portfolio: ~/blog
feed.subtitle: Writing about things I find interesting. Sometimes dev, sometimes not.

entry.title: arrow.el: Creating a unified bookmark system in Emacs.
entry.summary: Why I built arrow.el for emacs, a neovim plugin designed for mnemonic bookmarks.

entry.title: The final piece of the spaced-repetition puzzle.
entry.summary: Exploring how the spacing and testing effect can be extended to solving problems with long chain of thought.

entry.title: The missing part of existing Audio editors
entry.summary: Where audio editors like Audacity fail and why I built something better.

entry.title: Reclaiming re-reading and the Leitner box system for Emacs.
entry.summary: An Emacs org-mode solution for re-reading your buried org notes using spaced-repetition

entry.title: The iterative approach to notetaking (a better way to study)
entry.summary: An alternate and competing method of writing good notes based on real meta-learning studies

entry.title: Why I use Org-mode & Org-roam over Obsidian
entry.summary: A note on why I went back to Emacs and never looked back, even with all the friction.

entry.title: Building a Better Anki: Enhancing Flashcards for Multi-Step Active Recall
entry.summary: What I've learned from years of Anki and why I'm building something different.

entry.title: NixOS: three months in
entry.summary: Everything I wish someone had told me before I wiped Windows.

entry.title: MCTS vs Minimax when to explore, when to exploit
entry.summary: Notes from building a chess AI: why pure Minimax is boring and why MCTS alone isn't enough.
